name: hr_recruiting_knowledge_graph_schema
purpose: Constrain knowledge extraction and graph writes for the HR recruiting assistant. This schema prevents uncontrolled node/relationship creation and replaces scattered regex routing with typed knowledge.

node_types.label: Term
node_types.description: Controlled dictionary term, synonym group, typo variant or trucking/recruiting slang.

properties.category: One of greeting, faq_topic, dropoff_signal, dropoff_close_signal, profile_signal, safety_static, ambiguous_slang, sensitive_handoff, jargon.
properties.aliases: List of accepted variants. Matching is normalized outside Neo4j.
properties.meanings: Optional list of possible meanings for ambiguous slang.
properties.action: Optional controlled action such as clarify_or_dual_safe_response, answer_from_knowledge, handoff_review.

node_types.label: Intent
node_types.description: Business intent detected from terms or conversation state.

properties.topic: Optional topic id for RAG context selection.

node_types.label: Route
node_types.description: Technical/business route selected by the knowledge node.

properties.id: greeting, rag, profile, human_handoff, clarification, fallback, policy_boundary, candidate_dropoff_recovery.
properties.label: Human readable route label.

node_types.label: ReplyTemplate
node_types.description: Controlled static or semi-static candidate-facing reply.

node_types.label: Policy
node_types.description: Operational, compliance or safety policy that constrains generation.

properties.label: Policy display name.
properties.public_guidance: Short safe public guidance for the LLM/context builder.

node_types.label: InternalSource
node_types.description: Internal document or source id preferred for a term/topic.

node_types.label: Candidate
node_types.description: Candidate identity and current recruiting profile facts. Initially remains in Postgres; Neo4j relation is future-ready.

properties.id: Stable candidate id or conversation-derived id.
properties.name: Optional candidate full name.

node_types.label: Conversation
node_types.description: Conversation session state. Initially mirrored from Postgres.

relationship_types.description: A matched controlled term suggests a business intent.
Intent maps to the route selected by the knowledge node.
Preferred source for RAG retrieval/context building.
Controlled static reply for a matched term/intent/route.
Policy applies when this intent/topic/term is active.
Conversation/message mentioned a controlled term. Future audit edge.
Conversation was classified by the knowledge node into this intent. Future audit edge.
